layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ALBERT BANDURA

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: based on the assumption that anxiety ac-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: tivates defensive behavior (Wolpe, 1974).

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: According to this view, association of neutral

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: events with aversive stimulation creates an

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: anxiety drive that motivates defensive be-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: havior; the defensive behavior, in turn, is

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: reinforced by reducing the anxiety aroused

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: by conditioned aversive stimuli. Hence, to

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: eliminate defensive responding, it is con-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: sidered necessary to eradicate its underlying

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: anxiety. Treatment strategies are therefore

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: keyed to reduction of emotional arousal.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Aversive stimuli are presented at graduated

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: levels in conjunction with relaxation until

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: anxiety reactions to the threats are elimi-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: nated.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: approach behavior and for approach behavior

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: toward the dissimilar threat. These differ-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ential findings indicate that experienced

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: mastery altered subjects' sense of personal

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: efficacy rather than merely providing be-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: havioral cues for judgments of self-efficacy.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Comparison of Self-eficacy and Dual-Process

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Theory

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: The standard desensitization approach is

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: As a further test of the generality of the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: theory under discussion, a microanalysis was

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: conducted of efficacy expectations instated

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: by desensitization procedures, which are

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: aimed at reducing emotional arousal. Social

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: learning theory and the dual-process theory

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: of anxiety, on which the desensitization ap-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: proach is based, posit different explanatory

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: therefore give rise to differential predictions

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: that can be readily tested.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: mechanisms for the changes accompanying

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: this mode of treatment. The alternative views

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Although desensitization produces be-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: havioral changes, there is little evidence to

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: support the original rationale that defensive

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: behavior is diminished because anxiety is

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: eliminated either by reciprocal physiological

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: inhibition or by associative recoupling of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: threatening stimuli to relaxation. Desensitiza-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: tion does not require graduated exposure, and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: anxiety-reducing activities are at most facili-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: tory, not necessary, conditions for eliminating

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: defensive behavior (Bandura, 1969; Wilson

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: & Davison, 1971).

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: The theory also accounts for variations in

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: behavioral change produced by modeling

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: alone. To equate for duration of treatment,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: subjects in the modeling condition were

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: yoked to matched counterparts in participant

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: modeling, who received treatment until they

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: performed all the therapeutic tasks. The

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: subjects in the participant modeling condi-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: tion varied in the time they required to com-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: plete treatment, so some of the subjects in

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the modeling conditions had only brief ex-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: posure to successful performances, whereas

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: others had the benefit of observing feared

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: activities modeled repeatedly without any

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: untoward consequences. The findings are

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: consistent with hypothesized increases in self-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: efficacy as a function of repeated observa-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: tion of successful modeling. Brief exposure

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: produced limited increases in the level (9%)

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and strength (5%) of efficacy expectations

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and correspondingly little behavior change

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: (10%). In contrast, repeated observation of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: successful performances increased by a sub-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: stantial amount the level (44%) and strength

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: (38%) of self-efficacy which, in turn, was

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: accompanied by similarly large increments

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: in performance (35%).

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: The principal assumption that defensive

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: behavior is controlled by anxiety arousal is

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: also disputed by several lines of evidence.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Autonomic arousal, which constitutes the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: principal index of anxiety, is not necessary

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: for defensive learning. Because autonomic re-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: actions take much longer to activate than do

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: avoidance responses, the latter cannot be

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: caused by the former. Studies in which auto-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: nomic and avoidance responses are measured

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: concurrently indicate that these two modes

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: of activity may be partially correlated in the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: acquisition phase but are not causally re-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: lated (Black, 1965). Avoidance behavior, for

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: example, can persist long after autonomic

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: reactions to threats have been extinguished.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Surgical removal of autonomic feedback ca-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: pability in animals has little effect on the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: acquisition of avoidance responses (Rescorla

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: & Solomon, 1967). Maintenance of avoidance

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: behavior is even less dependent on autonomic

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: feedback. Once defensive behavior has been

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: learned, depriving animals of autonomic feed-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: In the social learning analysis presented

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: earlier, reducing physiological arousal im-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: proves performance by raising efficacy ex-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: pectations rather than by eliminating a drive

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: that instigates the defensive behavior. This

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: information-based view of the mediating

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: mechanism predicts that the higher and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: stronger the efficacy expectations instated by

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: desensitization procedures, the greater are

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the reductions in defensive behavior. Because

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: arousal is only one of several sources of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: efficacy information, and not necessarily the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: most dependable one, extinguishing anxiety

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: environment, not the stimuli, that are

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: changed by correlated experience. Stimuli

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: having predictive significance signal the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: likelihood of painful consequences unless

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: protective measures are taken. Defensive be-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: havior, in turn, is maintained by its success

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: in forestalling or reducing the occurrence of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: aversive events. Once established, self-pro-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: tective behavior is difficult to eliminate even

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: though the hazards no longer exist. This is

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: because consistent avoidance prevents a per-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: son from learning that the real-life conditions

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: have changed. Hence, the nonoccurrence of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: anticipated hazards reinforces the expectation

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: that the defensive maneuvers forestalled

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: them.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: back does not hasten the rate at which such

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: activities are extinguished.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: SELF-EFFICACY THEORY

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Social learning theory regards anxiety and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: defensive behavior as coeffects rather than as

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: causally linked (Bandura, 1977). Aversive

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: experiences, either of a personal or vicarious

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: sort, create expectations of injurious effects

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: that can activate both fear and defensive be-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: havior. Being coeffects, there is no fixed

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: relationship between autonomic arousal and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: actions. Until effective coping behaviors are

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: achieved, perceived threats produce high

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: emotional arousal and various defensive

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: maneuvers. But after people become adept at

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: self-protective behaviors, they perform them

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: in potentially threatening situations without

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: having to be frightened (Notterman et al.,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 1952). Should their habitual coping devices

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: fail, they experience heightened arousal until

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: new defensive learning reduces their vulner-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ability.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: From the perspective of dual-process

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: theory, thorough extinction of anxiety should

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: eliminate avoidance behavior. In the desensi-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: tization treatment, however, anxiety reactions

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: are typically extinguished to visualized rep-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: resentations of feared situations. One would

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: expect some transfer loss of extinction effects

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: from symbolic to real-life threats, as is in-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: deed the case (Agras, 1967; Barlow et al.,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 1969). It is not uncommon for people to

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: fear and avoid real-life situations to which

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: they have been desensitized in imagery.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Therefore, according to this view, thorough

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: extinction of anxiety to visualized threats

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: should produce substantial, though less than

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: complete, reductions in defensive behavior.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: However, dual-process theory provides no

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: basis for predicting either the level of be-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: havior change or the variability in behavior

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: displayed by subjects who have all been

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: equally desensitized.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Perceived threats activate defensive be-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: havior because of their predictive value rather

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: than their aversive quality. That is, when

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: formerly neutral stimuli are associated with

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: painful experiences, it is not that the stimuli

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: have become aversive but that individuals

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: have learned to anticipate aversive con-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: sequences. It is people's knowledge of their

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Research casts doubt on the postulated

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: reinforcement sources, as well as the activat-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ing sources, of defensive behavior. In the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: dual-process theory, the anxiety reduction

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: occasioned by escape from the feared stim-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ulus presumably reinforces the defensive be-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: havior. The evidence, however, reveals that

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: whether or not defensive behavior removes

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the feared stimulus has variable effects on

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the maintenance of the behavior (Bolles,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 1972a). Moreover, defensive behavior can be

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: acquired and maintained by its success in

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: diminishing the frequency of aversive stim-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ulation, even though there are no feared

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: stimuli to arouse anxiety and to provide the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: source of decremental reinforcement (Herrn-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: stein, 1969). The substantial negative evi-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: dence concerning an anxiety mediational

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: mechanism in avoidance behavior suggests

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: that the effects of desensitization treatment

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: must result from some other mechanism of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: operation.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ALBERT BANDURA

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Results of the microanalysis of congruence

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: between self-efficacy at the end of treatment

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and performance on each of the tasks ad-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ministered in the posttest are consistent with

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the findings obtained from enactive and vi-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: carious treatment. Self-efficacy was an ac-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: curate predictor of subsequent performance

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: on 85% for all the tasks, and 83% for the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: subset of tasks that subjects were unable to

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: perform in the pretest assessment. Subjects

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: successfully executed tasks within the range

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: of their perceived self-efficacy produced by

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the desensitization treatment, whereas they

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: failed at tasks they perceived to be beyond

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: their capabilities.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: efficacy at the end of treatment, the more

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: approach behavior they subsequently per-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: formed in the posttest assessment (r = .74).

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: The preceding series of experiments ex-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: amined the predictive value of self-efficacy at

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the completion of different modes of treat-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ment. A further study investigated the process

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: of efficacy and behavioral change during the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: course of treatment itself. Participant model-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: arousal is rarely a sufficient condition for

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: eliminating defensive behavior.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Microanalysis of Self-efficacy and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Performance During the Process of Change

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: To test the theory that desensitization

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: changes behavior through its intervening

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: effects on efficacy expectations, severe snake

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: phobics were administered the standard

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: desensitization treatment until their emo-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: tional reactions were completely extinguished

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: to imaginal representations of the most

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: aversive scenes (Bandura & Adams, in press).

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: The assessment procedures were identical to

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: those used in the preceding experiment. Sub-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: jects' approach behavior was tested on the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: series of performance tasks before and after

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the desensitization treatment. The level,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: strength, and generality of their efficacy

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: expectations were similarly measured before

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: treatment, upon completion of treatment but

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: prior to the posttest, and following the post-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: test.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: The findings show that phobics whose

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: anxiety reactions to visualized threats have

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: been thoroughly extinguished emerge from

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the desensitization treatment with widely

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: differing efficacy expectations. As depicted

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: graphically in Figure 5, performance cor-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: responds closely to level of self-efficacy. The

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: higher the subjects' level of perceived self-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Figure 5. Level of efficacy expectations and approach behavior displayed by subjects toward

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: different threats after their emotional reactions to symbolic representations of feared activities

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: were eliminated through systematic desensitization. (Bandura & Adams, in press.)

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Concluding Remarks

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ing was selected for this purpose because the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: amount of treatment can be well-regulated

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and it promotes rapid change.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: As in the previous studies, adults whose

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: lives were adversely affected by severe snake

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: phobias were tested for their efficacy expecta-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: tions and approach behavior using the micro-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: analytic methodology described earlier. The

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: various treatment activities were segmented

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: into natural blocks of tasks of increasing

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: difficulty and threat value. Items in the initial

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: block included looking at a snake from

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: progressively closer distances; intermediate

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: blocks required subjects to touch and to hold

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the snake with gloved and bare hands for

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: increasing intervals; the terminal block re-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: quired them to tolerate the snake crawling

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: about freely in their laps for an extended

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: period. Subjects received the participant

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: modeling treatment only for the block of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: items they failed in the hierarchy of assess-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ment tasks. Treatment was continued until

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: they could perform the activities in the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: failed block, whereupon they were tested for

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: their efficacy expectations and approach re-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: sponses on the succeeding tasks. Subjects

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: who attained terminal performances received

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: no further treatment. For those who achieved

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: only partial improvement, the sequence of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: treatment on the failed block followed by

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: assessments of self-efficacy and approach be-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: havior on succeeding blocks was repeated until

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: they achieved terminal performances.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: The present theoretical formulation orders

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: variations in the level of behavioral changes

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: produced by different modes of treatment; it

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: accounts for behavioral variations displayed

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: by individuals receiving the same type of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: treatment; and it predicts performance suc-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: cesses at the level of individual tasks during

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and after treatment. It is possible to generate

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: alternative explanations for particular sub-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: sets of data, but the mechanism proposed in

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the present theory appears to account equally

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: well for the different sets of findings. It might

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: be argued, for example, that self-efficacy

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: proved to be an accurate predictor of per-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: formance in the enactive mode of treatment

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: because subjects were simply judging their

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: future performance from their past behavior.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: However, an interpretation of this type has.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: no explanatory value for the vicarious and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: emotive treatments, in which perceived self-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: efficacy was an equally accurate predictor of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: performance although subjects engaged in no

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: overt behavior. Even in the enactive treat-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ment, perceived self-efficacy proved to be a

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: better predictor of behavior toward unfamiliar

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: threats than did past performance. More-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: over, self-efficacy derived from partial en-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: active mastery during the course of treatment

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: predicted performance on stressful tasks that

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the individuals had never done before.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: SELF-EFFICACY 'THEORY

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Findings of the microanalysis lend further

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: support to the postulated cognitive mecha-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: nism of change. Subjects who mastered the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: same intermediate performances during the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: course of treatment varied considerably in

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: their behavioral attainments when tested on

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: succeeding blocks of tasks. Past performance

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: was therefore of limited value in predicting

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: what subjects would be able to do when con-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: fronted with more threatening tasks. However,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: efficacy judgments proved to be good predic-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: tors of degree of behavioral change resulting

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: from partial mastery experiences. Self-efficacy

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: predicted subsequent performance as mea-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: sured at different points in treatment in 92%

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: of the total assessment tasks. This relationship

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: holds even when the measure of congruence is

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: based only on the subset of activities that

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: subjects could not perform in pretest because

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: they found them too threatening, and did not

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: perform in treatment because the activities

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: extended beyond the failed block. Expecta-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: tions of personal effectiveness formed through

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: partial mastery experiences during the course

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: of treatment predicted, at a 84% level of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: accuracy, performance on highly threatening

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: tasks that subjects had never done before.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: As an alternative explanation, one could

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: invoke a superordinate mediator that con-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: trols both efficacy expectations and behavior.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Although such a possibility is not inconceiv-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: able, the mediator would have to be an ex-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ceedingly complex one to account adequately

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: for the diverse sets of relationships. To cite

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: but a few examples, it would have to affect

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: differentially efficacy expectations and be-

layout_dets.text: Evidence that people develop somewhat

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: comment. One possible explanation for the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: variance is in terms of differential cognitive

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: processing of efficacy information. To the ex-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: tent that individuals differ in how they cog-

layout_dets.text: nitively appraise their arousal decrements and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: behavioral attainments, their percepts of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: self-efficacy will vary to some degree. A sec-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ond possibility concerns the multiple deter-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: mination of self-efficacy. Because people have

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: met with different types and amounts of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: efficacy-altering experiences, providing one

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: new source of efficacy information would not

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: be expected to affect everyone uniformly.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Thus, for example, extinguishing arousal to

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: threats will enhance self-efficacy, but more

layout_dets.label: ocr_text

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: have occasionally succeeded than in those who

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: have consistently failed.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text

layout_dets.label: ocr_text

layout_dets.text: different levels of self-efficacy from ´équiva-

layout_dets.text: lent reductions in emotional arousal; and it

layout_dets.label: ocr_text

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: accordingly.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: The research completed thus far has tested

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the predictive power of the conceptual scheme

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: for efficacy expectations developed through

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: enactive, vicarious, and emotive-based pro-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: cedures. Additional tests of the generality of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: this approach need to be extended to efficacy

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: expectations arising from verbal persuasion

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and from other types of treatments aimed at

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: reducing emotional arousal.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Cognitive processing of efficacy informa-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: tion, which is an important component func-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: tion in the proposed theory, is an especially

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: relevant area for research. A number of fac-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: tors were identified as influencing the cog-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: nitive appraisal of efficacy information con-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: veyed by each of the major sources of self-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: efficacy. Previous research from a number

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: The operative process involved in the rela-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: tionship between efficacy expectations and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: action also requires further investigation. It

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: will be recalled that efficacy expectations are

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: presumed to influence level of performance

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: by enhancing intensity and persistence of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: effort. In the preceding experiments, the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: behavioral tasks were ordered in level of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: difficulty and subjects either persisted in

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: their efforts until they completed all of the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: tasks or they quit at varying points along

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the way. The number of tasks successfully

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: completed reflects degree of perseverance. As

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: a further step toward elucidating the inter-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: vening process, it would be of interest to

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: measure the intensity and duration of effort

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: subjects exert in attempts to master arduous

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: or insoluble tasks as a function of the level

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and strength of their efficacy expectations.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Further research on the processes postulated

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: in the present theoretical formulation should

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: increase our understanding of the relation-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ship between cognitive and behavioral change.
